title: Malty Ukrainian Swede Borscht
description: A comforting and unique Ukrainian dish made with swede and featuring a malty, molten flavor profile that is also friendly to Ayurvedic rajasic principles.

ingredients.name: caraway seeds
ingredients.notes: add a slightly bitter, nutty flavor

ingredients.name: beetroot
ingredients.notes: optional, for a deeper red color and earthy flavor

ingredients.name: tobacco sauce
ingredients.notes: a traditional Ukrainian condiment, adds a unique flavor

instructions: Peel and chop the swede into large chunks, then boil until tender
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a pan and fry the chopped onion until caramelized
Add the caraway seeds, beetroot, and carrots to the pan and stir to combine
Add the beef broth to the pan and bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes
Mash the cooked swede with a fork until mostly smooth, then add to the pan and stir to combine
Stir in the molasses and tobacco sauce
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed
Serve hot, garnished with deep-fried onion if desired
To make the deep-fried onion, peel and chop a large onion into rings, then fry in hot oil until crispy and golden
